notes: Most-common shape — mid-market new vendor with PII and two open items. Tests routing breadth (4 approvers) and warn-not-block discrimination. The two open items (SOC 2 II pending, DPA pending) are correctable via vendor follow-up rather than blocking.
Floor case — low-risk renewal of an existing vendor with no data access. Tests that the agent does NOT escalate routine paperwork; W-9 is a warn (must-have for payment) but not a block.

Ceiling case — multi-blocker scenario combining restricted PII + AI training on customer data + missing security/legal artifacts + cross-border + Net 60. Tests escalation reasoning (3 distinct blockers compound; agent must not collapse them into one flag).

Negative-control case — sub-$10k renewal with complete docs and no data. Tests that the agent doesn't manufacture flags when there is nothing to flag. Should yield at most one info-severity routing flag (business owner).

Tests the middle-data tier (internal, not PII) — security review is required because integration touches CRM but no PII passes through. Single missing artifact (security questionnaire) keeps it in approve_with_followup.

Isolates restricted-PII risk without the AI confound — tests that high risk_tier alone does NOT trigger escalation when all blockers are resolved (SOC 2 II provided, DPA executed). Compares to case_003 where the same data tier WITH blockers → escalate.

Board-tier ACV ($500k+) with complete docs. Tests that escalation can be driven by ACV alone (Board approval required) even when no policy blockers exist. Stress-tests the routing layer (5 approvers including Board Finance Committee).

PHI-specific path — HIPAA requires a signed Business Associate Agreement before any PHI flows. Tests that the agent recognizes BAA as a distinct artifact from a generic DPA, and that PHI triggers compliance routing on top of legal/security.

Amendment to an existing contract — agent should skip full intake re-validation and focus on the delta. Tests that vendor_stage='amendment' suppresses the full security/legal sweep when no terms changed materially.

Discrimination test — AI training on customer data WITHOUT restricted-PII. Compares to case_003 where the same AI-training-opt-out gap was a block (because data was restricted). Here, internal-data + missing opt-out should be a warn (correctable via vendor reply), not a block.

Edge case — individual contractor (1099) not a corp. Tests that the agent surfaces the W-9 vs 1099 distinction and a Certificate of Insurance gap, but doesn't misclassify a short-term audit engagement as needing the full security review.
